{"key_event":"French Revolution and Terror","reasoning":"Civil society became extraordinarily mobilized through clubs, sections, newspapers, popular protest, and electoral politics, but remained fragmented and repeatedly suppressed, especially under the Terror and Directory. State power was high but unstable: revolutionary governments built mass conscription, surveillance, administrative centralization, and coercive capacity, yet civil war, fiscal crisis, and regime turnover limited consistent authority.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":7.0,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":7.0}
